message:
feat(fsfs): implement file classification contract with runtime classifier

Major expansion of the file classification module (~890 new lines) to
implement the fsfs-file-classification-contract v1:

- Add kind/schema constants and 30+ classification label constants
  (FSFS_TEXT_*, FSFS_BINARY_*, FSFS_PARTIAL_*, FSFS_CORRUPT_*)
- Implement runtime FileClassifier with classify() method that performs:
  * BOM detection (UTF-8/UTF-16 LE/BE)
  * Null-byte binary detection with configurable thresholds
  * Archive extension blocking via configurable blocklist
  * High-bit ratio heuristic for encoding detection
  * UTF-8 validation with NFC normalization support
  * Truncation detection with configurable max_prefix_bytes
- Add ClassificationDecision event struct for per-file verdicts
- Add CorruptFileEvent for checksum/decode/IO error reporting
- Add impl blocks for SniffHeuristics, NormalizationPolicy,
  TruncatedAction, ChecksumMismatchAction with accessor methods
- Comprehensive unit tests for all classification paths

Also includes cross-crate changes:
- host_adapter: rustfmt formatting fixes for golden JSON assertions
- types, schema_conformance: minor formatting adjustments
- file_protector, model_manifest, bead_self_doc, telemetry_transport:
  derive/formatting corrections
- searcher: formatting cleanup in search pipeline
- in_memory: minor adjustment
- ops/simulator: formatting fix
- rerank: formatting cleanup
- Beads issue tracker flush

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-yrq","title":"Embedding-Stable Document Fingerprinting","description":"Implement content-aware document fingerprinting that detects when a document has changed enough to warrant re-embedding. This bridges the content-hash deduplication layer (bd-3w1.4) and the staleness detector (bd-3w1.12) with embedding-aware change detection.

## Key Insight

Not all document changes affect embedding quality equally. A typo fix doesn't change semantic meaning, but adding a new paragraph does. We need a fingerprint that is stable under minor edits but sensitive to semantic changes.

## Design

```rust
pub struct DocumentFingerprint {
    pub content_hash: u64,           // Full content FNV-1a hash (exact change detection)
    pub semantic_hash: u64,          // SimHash of content (approximate semantic similarity)
    pub char_count: u32,             // Character count (length change detection)
    pub token_estimate: u32,         // Estimated token count (whitespace split)
}

impl DocumentFingerprint {
    pub fn compute(text: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn needs_reembedding(&self, other: &Self, threshold: f64) -> bool;
}
```

## SimHash Algorithm (Charikar 2002)

1. Tokenize text into shingles (3-word windows)
2. Hash each shingle with FNV-1a
3. For each bit position, sum +1 for 1-bits, -1 for 0-bits
4. Final hash: bit i = 1 if sum_i > 0, else 0
5. Hamming distance between SimHashes ≈ 1 - cosine similarity of shingle sets

## Re-Embedding Decision Logic

- If content_hash differs AND hamming_distance(semantic_hash_a, semantic_hash_b) > threshold (default: 8/64 bits = 12.5%): re-embed
- If only content_hash differs but semantic_hash is close: skip re-embedding (minor edit)
- If char_count change > 20%: always re-embed (significant length change)

## Integration with Staleness Detector (bd-3w1.12)

- Store DocumentFingerprint in FrankenSQLite alongside document metadata
- Staleness detector compares stored fingerprints with current content
- Report: "N documents need re-embedding (M changed significantly, K minor edits skipped)"

## Why This Matters

Re-embedding is the most expensive operation in frankensearch (128ms per document for quality tier). For xf with 50K tweets, a full re-index takes ~107 minutes. Smart fingerprinting can skip 60-80% of documents that had only minor edits (e.g., metadata updates, formatting changes), reducing re-index time to ~20-40 minutes.

## Testing

- Unit: identical text → identical fingerprint
- Unit: minor typo → same semantic_hash, different content_hash
- Unit: significant change → different semantic_hash
- Unit: needs_reembedding threshold logic
- Unit: char_count/token_estimate accuracy
- Integration: fingerprint round-trip through FrankenSQLite storage
- Benchmark: fingerprint computation throughput (should be >100K docs/sec)","acceptance_criteria":"1. The feature is fully implemented according to this bead design, scope, and referenced files or modules.
2. Integration with dependent components is complete and behavior remains correct across normal and degraded flows.
3. Edge and error cases are explicitly handled with deterministic outcomes and clear reason codes or errors.
4. Unit and integration tests (and e2e where relevant) are added, passing, and include detailed structured logging assertions.
5. Performance and resource expectations in the bead are validated and documented with reproducible evidence.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:00:14.096481882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:15:20.618396190Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T04:15:20.618372165Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yrq","depends_on_id":"bd-3un","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:12:48.530495352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yrq","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:02:23.773738815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yrq","depends_on_id":"bd-3w1.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:02:23.995892867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yrq","depends_on_id":"bd-3w1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:02:23.884886689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":308,"issue_id":"bd-yrq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REFINEMENT PASS 1: No asupersync needed -- DocumentFingerprint::compute() is a pure synchronous function (SimHash computation, FNV-1a hashing, character counting). The needs_reembedding() comparison is also pure computation. Storage integration (FrankenSQLite) is synchronous SQLite access. BODY QUALITY: The bead is well-specified with clear algorithm description (SimHash/Charikar 2002), concrete re-embedding decision logic with three criteria (hamming distance, semantic hash closeness, char count change). The 3-word shingle window for SimHash is a good choice for semantic stability.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:07:35Z"},{"id":336,"issue_id":"bd-yrq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REFINEMENT PASS 4 (testing gaps): Missing edge-case tests:
- [ ] Unit: empty string input -- DocumentFingerprint::compute("") should produce deterministic zero/default values
- [ ] Unit: very long document (1MB+) -- verify SimHash performance does not degrade unexpectedly and shingle count is reasonable
- [ ] Unit: Unicode/non-ASCII text (CJK, emoji, RTL) -- verify 3-word shingle tokenization handles multi-byte correctly
- [ ] Unit: document with only whitespace -- edge case for token_estimate
- [ ] Unit: SimHash collision resistance -- two semantically different documents should have Hamming distance > threshold
- [ ] Unit: needs_reembedding symmetry -- needs_reembedding(a, b) == needs_reembedding(b, a)
- [ ] Integration: bulk fingerprint computation (10K documents) matches expected throughput (>100K docs/sec from body)
All existing 7 tests are well-specified. These additions harden the edge cases around text processing.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:18:31Z"},{"id":352,"issue_id":"bd-yrq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Cross-reference: bd-2hz.2.5 (fsfs incremental change-detection contract) defines filesystem-level change detection (mtime, inode, content hash) for the fsfs product. This bead's SimHash fingerprinting provides a complementary semantic-level signal: 'has the document's meaning changed enough to warrant re-embedding?' fsfs could use yrq fingerprints to skip expensive re-embedding when file content changes are cosmetic (whitespace, formatting) but semantically unchanged. This optimization becomes important at scale (100K+ files) where re-embedding every changed file is prohibitive.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:20:40Z"},{"id":390,"issue_id":"bd-yrq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CRATE PLACEMENT: frankensearch-core (DocumentFingerprint is a utility type with no external dependencies beyond FNV-1a hashing, which is already in core. New file: core/src/fingerprint.rs)","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:50:51Z"},{"id":785,"issue_id":"bd-yrq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented DocumentFingerprint in core with deterministic exact+semantic change detection.

Code:
- Added `crates/frankensearch-core/src/fingerprint.rs` with:
  - `DocumentFingerprint { content_hash, semantic_hash, char_count, token_estimate }`
  - `compute(text)` using FNV-1a content hash + 64-bit SimHash over 3-token shingles
  - `semantic_hamming_distance`, `semantic_distance_ratio`, `char_count_delta_ratio`
  - `needs_reembedding(other, threshold)` with 3-rule decision logic
  - constants `DEFAULT_SEMANTIC_CHANGE_THRESHOLD` (8/64) and `SIGNIFICANT_CHAR_COUNT_CHANGE_THRESHOLD` (20%)
- Exported fingerprint API from `crates/frankensearch-core/src/lib.rs`.
- Re-exported from facade/prelude in `frankensearch/src/lib.rs` for consumer access.

Tests:
- Added 10 focused unit tests in `fingerprint.rs` covering:
  - identical text stability
  - formatting-only changes (content hash changes, semantic hash stable)
  - significant semantic changes
  - threshold sensitivity
  - char-count trigger >20%
  - token/char accounting
  - empty + whitespace inputs
  - unicode inputs
  - symmetry of re-embedding decision
  - large-document handling

Validation evidence:
- `cargo test -p frankensearch-core fingerprint -- --nocapture` ✅ (10/10 pass)
- `cargo +nightly test -p frankensearch-core --all-targets` ✅ (229/229 pass)
- `cargo check -p frankensearch-core --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo +nightly check -p frankensearch --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo +nightly check --workspace --all-targets` ❌ pre-existing failure in `crates/frankensearch-tui/src/screen.rs:144` (lifetime issue)
- `cargo +nightly clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ❌ pre-existing warnings/errors in `frankensearch-embed`, `frankensearch-index`, `frankensearch-fsfs`
- `cargo +nightly fmt --check` ❌ pre-existing formatting drift in `crates/frankensearch-tui/**`
- `ubs --only=rust --diff` ✅ exit 0 (no critical UBS blockers)
","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:15:16Z"}]}
